description: Telegram AI Digest — fetches messages from Telegram channels via Telethon, summarises them with Claude, and publishes a self-hosted HTML digest page. The page URL is sent to a private Telegram channel. Entry point: python digest.py. Tests: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v.

testingPolicy.rule: Any story touching fetch_messages, build_html_page, or extract_* functions requires BOTH (a) unit tests green AND (b) python digest.py --dry-run run on the production machine with the output HTML manually inspected before passes may be set to true.
testingPolicy.rationale: Unit tests verify code paths but cannot catch layout issues, missing media labels, or broken RTL rendering in the actual browser output.
testingPolicy.checklist: Before setting passes:true, tick off each acceptance criterion against the rendered HTML file and record confirmation in the story's notes field.

userStories.title: Media-aware fetch + source map
acceptanceCriteria: extract_media_info(message) returns ('video', duration_sec) for video messages, ('photo', None) for photos, ('document', None) for other documents, (None, None) for text-only
extract_external_links(message) returns only non-t.me URLs from message.entities; handles None entities; handles MessageEntityUrl and MessageEntityTextUrl; preserves order of first appearance
fetch_messages returns a tuple (message_strings, source_map) — message_strings for Claude, source_map dict keyed by t.me permalink
Video-only messages are no longer silently dropped — they appear in the Claude input with [VIDEO: M:SS] [סרטון]
Video + caption messages appear with [VIDEO: M:SS] {caption}
Image-only messages appear with [IMAGE] [תמונה]
Image + caption messages appear with [IMAGE] {caption}
Each source_map entry contains: text (str), media_type (str|None), video_duration (int|None), external_links (list[str])
main() accumulates source_map across all channels into a single dict
All tests pass: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v

userStories.title: HTML digest page + dry-run
acceptanceCriteria: build_html_page(digest, source_map, end_date) is a pure function — no file I/O, no network calls — returning a complete self-contained HTML string
Page is <!DOCTYPE html> with lang='he' dir='rtl' and all CSS inlined (no external dependencies)
Four sections rendered in order: conflict, politics, world, deep — empty sections omitted
Big news items have: h4 heading, meta line (channel + time), AI summary paragraph, קישור למקור link, and a <details>/<summary> source bubble
Minor news items are a <ul> where each <li> is a <details> element — summary is the headline, expanded content is the source bubble
Source bubbles are collapsed by default
Source bubble shows: channel name (bold), timestamp, original message text, 📹 M:SS label for video or 🖼 label for image, and a פתח בטלגרם link to the t.me permalink
External link from source_map entry used as קישור למקור when present; t.me link used as fallback when not
When a digest item has multiple links (merged messages), one bubble is rendered per link
Telegraph code fully removed: publish_to_telegraph, TOKEN_FILE, Telegraph import, telegraph from requirements.txt
--dry-run argparse flag added: generates HTML, saves to HTML_OUTPUT_DIR, logs path, skips send_message
HTML_OUTPUT_DIR and PUBLIC_BASE_URL read from .env (required)
HTML filename pattern: digest-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.html (local time from end_date)
Public URL returned: PUBLIC_BASE_URL/digest-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.html
All tests pass: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v
python digest.py --dry-run runs on the production machine and the output HTML is manually inspected in a browser

userStories.title: README: VPS server setup instructions
acceptanceCriteria: TELEGRAPH_TOKEN removed from all .env examples in README
HTML_OUTPUT_DIR and PUBLIC_BASE_URL documented in the .env setup section with example values
Web server setup section added covering nginx (static-file location block) and Python http.server as options
--dry-run flag documented under Usage: what it does and when to use it
Docker run example updated to mount HTML_OUTPUT_DIR as a volume
No references to Telegraph, Instant View, or telegraph_token remain in README
Feature list updated to describe HTML page output instead of Telegraph
